LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S MOTION FOR ORDER ENTERING JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT PURSUANT TO CCP 664.6 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS ARE ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN THEIR FAVOR AGAINST DEFENDANT C SUNCIN IN THE AMOUNT OF $17,242.56 PLUS COSTS. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-07-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Entering Judgment Against Deft Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
Off Calendar Sep-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Vivian Leung Lerche, Esq. (415-680-5731) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S Motion For Order Entering Judgment Against Deft Pursuant To CCP 664.6 is GRANTED. Plaintiffs are entitled to judgment in their favor against defendant C Suncin in the amount of $17,242.56 plus costs. No opposition filed. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Alisa J. Kim, Esq. (510-427-1158) appearas as counsel for defendant for plaintiff/moving party, American Express Centurion Bank

No other appearance is made in this matter

Court reads tentative ruling into the record for the benefit of plaitniff's counsel. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT is Denied without prejudice to being refiled with a proper proof of service. The proof of service shows that Mr. Suncin was served with the motion papers on Mr. Siroka. Although Mr. Siroka appears to have been Mr. Suncin's attorney at the time of the settlement agreement and he also signed that agreement, Mr. Siroka has never appeared as counsel for Mr. Suncin in this case and thus is not, absent a stipulation, a person for whom service can be made on Mr. Suncin.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)
